_about.title: G7 completion — ui-field (the label/description/error wrapper) + ui-form-provider (form-level coordination; the first context/provider primitive)
_about.serves: .claude/docs/goals.md §G7 — the two OPEN items ('ui-field NOT built', 'form-provider NOT built') + the final G7 DoD box: a provider-coordinated, keyboard-only end-to-end form (button + text-field + checkbox + switch + select inside a ui-field, under a form-provider) round-trips in the browser. Generalizes text-field's inline label/message seam (text-field.md: 'the visible label/description/error wrapper is ui-field at G7'; label → editor aria-label).
_about.method: system-decompose, components domain (COMPOSE = OUTSIDE-IN structure: slice groups → file-disjoint leaf slices; REALIZE = INSIDE-OUT actions: the G7 capabilities/acceptance, independent of the structure). `hosts` crosses them; coverage_check.py --strict is the gate. This is the INTAKE decomposition — the LLD is authored AFTER the two forks below are ratified (the fork calls change slice s1's shape).
_about.ground_state: G0–G6 + G9 + the Control Suite (Waves 0–5) DONE. UIFormElement (dom/form.ts) ships value/validity via ElementInternals, the setCustomValidity seam (ADR-0029), effectiveDisabled, and the form lifecycle hooks — every value control ALREADY natively form-associates with an owning <form>, so ui-form-provider is an AGGREGATION/COORDINATION layer on top of native association, never a replacement for <form>. The dom barrel exports element hosts / props / directives / mount — NO context primitive exists (verified 2026-07-01): form-provider introduces the first one. trackUserInvalid (traits/track-user-invalid.ts) owns the user-invalid TIMING; text-field composes it and manages its own aria-describedby message node — ui-field must COMPOSE with that seam, not fight it. Custom states are externally matchable (control.matches(':state(user-invalid)')) — the field can observe validity event-driven with zero new control API. The house is LIGHT-DOM by default, so cross-element id references (aria-labelledby) resolve without shadow-boundary machinery.
_about.decisions_to_ratify: TWO forks gate the build; both surfaced to Kim, neither self-settled. FORK 1 (the ADR — .claude/docs/adr/0050, proposed): the context/provider MECHANISM — (a) the community context-request protocol, (b) a connect-time registration event + reactive registry [RECOMMENDED], (c) querySelectorAll + MutationObserver scan. Decision analysis + alternatives live in ADR-0050; ratify before s1. FORK 2 (flagged HERE, second ADR when Kim picks): the label→control A11Y ASSOCIATION — native <label for> cannot reach a custom control's internal role-carrying part. (A) prop-sync: the field writes the slotted control's existing `label` prop from its own label text — zero new seam, works TODAY for text-field, but ONLY for controls that carry a label prop, and flattens rich label content to text. (B) a labelling-forwarding seam on UIFormElement [RECOMMENDED]: the field assigns ids to its label/description/error nodes and hands them to the control via a small documented base-level surface; each control forwards to its role-carrying part (text-field → the editor's aria-labelledby/aria-describedby; internals-role controls → internals ARIA element reflection, cross-engine support verified by the browser gate). Additive and uniform; costs one forwarding wire per control. (C) the field reaches into [data-part] directly — REJECTED (crosses the control's ownership boundary; parts are control-owned). Recommendation: B, with A as the bridge for controls not yet wired.
_about.field_error_composition: NOT a fork (mechanism is settled by existing seams): the field observes the slotted control EVENT-DRIVEN — input/change bubble, blur/invalid via capture, plus form reset — then re-reads control.matches(':state(user-invalid)') (the trackUserInvalid timing, honored for free) and control.validationMessage, rendering the visible error node only when user-invalid. The describedBy STITCHING (the field's error id joining/yielding to the control's own internal message node so AT hears ONE error, not two) is an LLD detail under fork 2's chosen seam.
_about.provider_and_native_form: The provider does NOT create or replace a native <form> (the zero-native rule bars native form WIDGETS; the owning <form> is what FACE association is FOR — the G4 browser proof uses one). ui-form-provider composes WITH an optional native <form> (wrapping it or sitting inside): submit = aggregate the registry (values/validity) and emit; reset = delegate to the native form.reset() when present, else formReset the registry members. A2UI integration (its checks controller + submit surface) is an INTEGRATION POINT ONLY — out of this intake's build scope.
_about.naming: Tags/classes per house law: ui-field / UIFieldElement (controls/field/ — the plan §3 reserved folder) and ui-form-provider / UIFormProviderElement (controls/form-provider/). Both extend UIElement (NEITHER is UIFormElement — neither carries a form VALUE; wrapping them formAssociated would double-submit; neither is UIContainerElement — no elevation/brightness surface paint). The registration event (fork 1b) is a PREFIXED protocol event (e.g. ui-form-connect), deliberately OUTSIDE the public component-event vocab (change·input·select·open·close·toggle) — it is plumbing between base and provider, not a consumer-facing semantic event; ADR-0050 records this.

_build_legend.now: buildable once the forks are ratified
_build_legend.prep: serial prerequisite that unblocks the fan-out
_build_legend.fan-out: file-disjoint with its wave siblings — one writer per file, parallel-safe
_build_legend.integration: the LONE shared-tree slice (barrels) — every cross-slice edit deferred here, serial
_build_legend.judgment-gate: verified by the component-reviewer's score / Kim's ratification, not a script

_first_slice: s0 — Kim ratifies ADR-0050 (fork 1) + picks fork 2; THEN the LLD is authored; THEN s1 (the base seams) is the serial prep every control slice depends on.

_waves.A-ratify: s0 (ADR-0050 fork-1 ratification + the fork-2 call + the post-fork LLD) — Kim's gate; nothing builds before it
_waves.B-prep: s1 (dom/form.ts registration dispatch + traits/form-registry.ts + the fork-2 labelling seam) — SERIAL prep
_waves.C-controls: s2 (field.ts) ∥ s3 (field.css) ∥ s4 (field.md) ∥ s5 (form-provider.ts) ∥ s6 (form-provider.css) ∥ s7 (form-provider.md) — file-disjoint fan-out against the fixed LLD interface  [after B]
_waves.D-probes: s8 (field.test.ts) ∥ s9 (form-provider.test.ts) ∥ s10 (descriptor trip-wires) ∥ s11 (browser smokes per control) ∥ s15 (the /site pages + TOCs — the drift-gate requirement, docs-writer)  [after C]
_waves.E-integration: s12 (barrels + component-styles + size/tree-shake) — the LONE serial integration slice  [after C]
_waves.F-e2e: s13 (the G7 DoD end-to-end proof — the provider-coordinated keyboard-only form, Chromium AND WebKit)  [after D,E]
_waves.G-score: s14 (component-reviewer scores BOTH controls ≥4 both axes — the per-control promotion gate)  [after ALL]

_predicted_gates.standing: npm run check && npm test → green on every code/probe slice. NOTE: full-suite green INCLUDES the site gates below once the two NEW descriptors exist — the D-wave 'full green restores' claim therefore needs s15, not just s8–s11
_predicted_gates.site: descriptor/site-coverage.test.ts + site-toc.test.ts (ride npm test) + check:site (folded into npm run check) — tier=container ⇒ {doc, demo} page per NEW descriptor + entries in BOTH TOCs (NAV in site/pages/_page.ts · CARD_GROUPS in site/main.ts); KNOWN_UNDOCUMENTED is deliberately EMPTY by that gate's own contract (shipped-but-undocumented is not a legal state), so the s15 pages are REQUIRED this wave, not deferrable
_predicted_gates.browser: npm run test:browser → Chromium AND WebKit green (s11 per-control smokes; s13 the end-to-end DoD proof)
_predicted_gates.drift: the contract↔props trip-wires (s10) — field.md/form-provider.md attributes[] ≡ finalize(...); a drifted attribute FAILS
_predicted_gates.size: npm run size (manual, ADR-0040 §3) — both marginals within the 22 KB family budget (ADR-0049); tree-shake clean
_predicted_gates.score: component-reviewer verdict COMPOSE≥4 AND REALIZE≥4 per control, zero [gate] fails (s14)

nodes.label: G7 completion — ui-field + ui-form-provider
fork ratification + the post-fork LLD (Kim's gate)
ADR-0050 (fork 1, proposed → Kim ratifies) + the fork-2 labelling-seam call + the LLD authored from the settled forks
nodes.dep: none — this intake authors the ADR proposed; Kim ratifies both forks; the LLD follows the calls
nodes.acceptance: ADR-0050 passes adr_check.py and is ratified by Kim (status flipped by the ratifier, NEVER self-flipped); fork 2 is decided (A/B/C per _about.decisions_to_ratify) and recorded (a second ADR if the seam touches UIFormElement's public surface); the LLD pins the interfaces (props/events/parts/registration detail shape) the C-wave fan-out builds against.

nodes.label: the base seams (dom + traits prep)

dom/form.ts — the connect-time registration dispatch (fork 1b) + traits/form-registry.ts — the reactive registry controller + the fork-2 labelling seam (+ the text-field editor-forwarding wire, same serial writer — LLD-C2)
nodes.dep: s0 (the forks are ratified; the exact seam shape is the LLD's)
nodes.acceptance: Per the ratified fork 1: UIFormElement announces itself at connect (the composed, bubbling protocol event with a connection-scoped teardown handle in the detail — a detached control deregisters via the handle, since a disconnected node cannot dispatch to the provider); the registry controller (traits/) holds a reactive set of registered controls, aggregation computeds read each control's PUBLIC reactive surface (name/value/validity — props are signals, so aggregation is fine-grained with zero polling), teardown is zero-residue (the trait contract). Per the ratified fork 2: the labelling seam lands (base-level, forwarded per control; text-field wires editor forwarding). Per the coordinator-ruled upgrade-path complement (ADR-0051 cl.5): UIFormElement also ships the public announceFormConnect() re-announce (re-dispatch iff the CONNECTION SIGNAL is live — a lifecycle guard, NOT structural isConnected: on a bulk subtree insert every descendant is isConnected before any connectedCallback runs, so a structural guard announces null handles [the s9 crash]; a same-cascade control self-covers via its own connect dispatch — fresh detail off that live signal; upgrade follows define order, not tree order). Imports stay inward-only; the layering trip-wire stays green.

nodes.label: ui-field (the wrapper control)

controls/field/field.ts — UIFieldElement: label/description/error parts, the slotted-control association (fork 2), reactive validity observation (the seam's closures), user-invalid-gated error display
nodes.acceptance: extends UIElement (carries NO form value); typed props (label/description at minimum — literal-union sizes if sized); control-owned label/description/error PARTS + the default slot for ONE form control; associates the label/description with the slotted control per the ratified fork-2 seam (late-slotted control handled; plus the ONE-SHOT upgrade-path catch-up at connect — announceFormConnect on descendant UIFormElements, ADR-0051 cl.5 — so a field defined/upgraded AFTER its control still associates; first-wins guard keeps it idempotent); observes the control REACTIVELY via the association detail's closures (a scope-owned effect over userInvalid()/validity() — coordinator re-ruled after the s11 UA-checkpoint finding killed both event-ordering generations; no observation listeners, no ordering/phase/task-timing dependence; the LLD's three-generation record holds the mechanics) and shows the visible error ONLY while the control is user-invalid (the observable: error visible ⟺ control.matches(':state(user-invalid)'), browser-asserted) with the verdict message (≡ control.validationMessage, but signal-tracked) as the text (the trackUserInvalid timing honored for free — the field adds NO second timing source); the describedBy stitching yields ONE AT-announced error (no double-announce with the control's internal message node); required/disabled visual affordances read the CONTROL's state (no duplicated source of truth); host carries no role/aria attributes; self-defines ui-field.

nodes.label: controls/field/field.css — the single sheet: :where token block + @scope styles, label typography, error ink, spacing
nodes.dep: s2 (part names fixed by the LLD — parallel-safe)
nodes.acceptance: single field.css (ADR-0003): :where(ui-field) declares --ui-field-* from --md-sys-color-{family}-{role} roles (error ink = the danger family; description = the muted on-surface variant) + spacing off the dimensional system; @scope (ui-field) consumes ONLY --ui-field-*; column flow (label / control / description / error); error visibility keyed off the field's own state hook; forced-colors keeps label/description/error ink visible; motion (error appearance) gated + reduced-motion zeroed. Geometry class: STRUCTURE (not the Control band — no fixed control height); the label rides the type ramp.

nodes.label: controls/field/field.md — the frontmatter descriptor + /site body (ADR-0004) + the text-field.md cross-reference repair (same writer)
nodes.acceptance: frontmatter mirrors static props (the trip-wire target); slots (the default control slot + any label/description slots per the LLD); parts (label/description/error); the fork-2 association mechanism documented; aria section records the labelling path + the single-error-announce composition; text-field.md's 'ui-field at G7' cross-reference is UPDATED to point here (repair the owning doc).
nodes.gate: npm run check && npm test (s10 parses it)

nodes.label: ui-form-provider (the coordination control)
controls/form-provider/form-provider.ts — UIFormProviderElement: the registry consumer, reactive aggregation, submit/reset
nodes.acceptance: extends UIElement; consumes the s1 registry controller: listens for the registration protocol event, registers/deregisters via the connection-scoped handle (a LATE-ADDED control registers by construction — its connectedCallback dispatches; NO MutationObserver needed under fork 1b; plus the ONE-SHOT upgrade-path catch-up at connect — announceFormConnect on descendant UIFormElements, ADR-0051 cl.5 — so a provider defined/upgraded AFTER its controls still discovers them; the registry dup guard keeps it idempotent); exposes the reactive aggregate (values by name · invalid set · overall validity) as a typed public surface; submit = aggregate + emit (composes with a native <form> when present — never creates a native form widget); reset = native form.reset() delegation when wrapped, else formReset on registry members + trackUserInvalid reset semantics hold (no post-reset danger flash); disconnect → zero residue (registry drops, aggregation computeds die with the scope); self-defines ui-form-provider.

nodes.label: controls/form-provider/form-provider.css — minimal structural sheet
nodes.acceptance: single sheet per ADR-0003 (a coordination element is near-invisible: display block + any status affordance tokens); :where/@scope discipline + sectioning hold even at minimal size (the token-hygiene probes don't special-case it).

nodes.label: controls/form-provider/form-provider.md — the frontmatter descriptor + /site body
nodes.acceptance: frontmatter mirrors static props; events (the aggregate submit surface — named within the house event vocab); the registration protocol documented (the ADR-0050 mechanism, the prefixed protocol event named as plumbing); the native-<form> composition rule recorded.
nodes.gate: npm run check && npm test (s10 parses it)

nodes.label: verification (jsdom + descriptor + browser)
field.test.ts — jsdom probes for the wrapper
nodes.acceptance: the label associates with the slotted control per the fork-2 seam (probe reads the control-side wiring); a LATE-slotted control gets associated; the error node is EMPTY pre-interaction even when the control is invalid (the user-invalid timing negative control), shows control.validationMessage after blur/change-invalid, clears when valid; ONE AT-announced error (the stitching probe); reset clears the visible error; connect→disconnect zero residue; a @ts-expect-error negative control on any literal-union prop.

nodes.label: form-provider.test.ts — jsdom probes for the coordination layer
nodes.acceptance: controls present at connect are discovered; a LATE-ADDED control is discovered (registration-on-connect — the goals.md MutationObserver requirement is satisfied by the ratified mechanism, asserted by behavior not implementation); a REMOVED control deregisters (the teardown-handle path) and leaves the aggregate; the aggregate value/validity map is REACTIVE (an effect reading it re-runs on a member control's value write — the props-are-signals proof); submit aggregates + emits; reset restores members and re-suppresses user-invalid; disconnect → zero residue; NEGATIVE controls: a non-UIFormElement child never registers; two providers nested — a control registers with the NEAREST only.

nodes.label: field-descriptor.test.ts + form-provider-descriptor.test.ts — the schema + contract↔props trip-wires
nodes.acceptance: both .md frontmatters validate against the schema; attributes[] ≡ finalize(class) bijection per control; NEGATIVE control: a planted drifted attribute FAILS (the trip-wire bites).

nodes.label: field + form-provider browser smokes — Chromium AND WebKit
nodes.acceptance: in BOTH engines: the fork-2 labelling path yields a real accessible name on the control's role-carrying part (the AX read-back — the reason fork 2 needs the browser gate, esp. any internals ARIA element reflection); the visible error appears post-blur-invalid and survives forced-colors (danger ink does not vanish); the provider aggregate tracks a real typed input; the WHOLE-SHAPE assertion (the test-the-whole-shape law): a ui-field with label+control+error renders a plausible bounding box in a realistic container, not a collapsed sliver.

nodes.label: the /site docs pages (drift-gated)
site pages — field-{doc,demo}.html + form-provider-{doc,demo}.html + both TOCs (NAV + CARD_GROUPS) — the site drift-gate requirement (docs-writer)
nodes.dep: s4, s7 (the descriptors the pages derive from) + s2, s5 (live controls for the demos)
nodes.acceptance: the 4 pages are DERIVED from the two descriptors (field.md / form-provider.md — not hand-written duplicates); BOTH TOCs (the NAV rail in site/pages/_page.ts + the CARD_GROUPS landing grid in site/main.ts) gain the ui-field and ui-form-provider groups; the site drift gates go green (site-coverage.test.ts + site-toc.test.ts — KNOWN_UNDOCUMENTED stays empty per that gate's contract: shipped-but-undocumented is not a legal state, so these pages are REQUIRED this wave); npm run check (incl. check:site) green.

nodes.label: packaging / integration (the lone serial slice)
barrels — traits/index.ts (+form-registry) · controls/index.ts (+field, +form-provider) · component-styles.css (@imports) + size/tree-shake
nodes.acceptance: the LONE shared-tree slice: registry trait + both controls exported; the two @imports added; any fleet-wide enumerating test (site-coverage / component-descriptor-source) updated HERE; npm run check && npm test green; npm run size — both marginals within the 22 KB budget (ADR-0049), tree-shake clean (importing ui-field does NOT drag the provider, and vice versa); the layering trip-wire green.
nodes.gate: npm run check && npm test && npm run size (manual)

nodes.label: the G7 DoD proof
the provider-coordinated, KEYBOARD-ONLY end-to-end form — the final open G7 DoD box, cross-engine
nodes.acceptance: in Chromium AND WebKit, KEYBOARD-ONLY: a form of button + text-field + checkbox + switch + select, each inside a ui-field, under one ui-form-provider — Tab order traverses every control; each control operates by keyboard (type/Space/Arrow/Enter per its contract); an invalid field shows its visible error post-interaction; fixing it clears; the provider aggregate matches the entered values; submit round-trips the aggregate; reset restores + re-suppresses errors. THIS closes goals §G7's final box ('the first component family is shippable').

nodes.label: the promotion gate
component-reviewer scores ui-field AND ui-form-provider — both axes ≥4 each (the per-control G7 promotion gate)
nodes.acceptance: the component-reviewer reviews each control against .claude/docs/rubrics/component.md + its contract: COMPOSE ≥4 AND REALIZE ≥4, zero [gate] fails, BEFORE the wave-commit (the load-bearing pre-commit discipline — jsdom-green ≠ done). goals §G7 is then flipped DONE (repair the owning doc in the same change).

actions.label: a UIFormElement announces itself to the nearest provider at connect (the ratified fork-1 mechanism); a late-added control is discovered by construction
a removed/disconnected control deregisters via the connection-scoped handle — the registry never holds a dead node; zero residue (the trait contract)
the context primitive respects the layer law — dom-level dispatch, traits-level registry, control-level consumption; imports point inward only
the field's visible label reaches the slotted control's role-carrying part as its accessible name (the ratified fork-2 seam; late-slotted handled)
description + error join the control's accessible description; AT hears exactly ONE error (the stitching — no double-announce with the control's internal message node)

the visible error is user-invalid-gated — hidden pre-interaction even when invalid, shown with control.validationMessage after blur/change, cleared when valid or on reset (composes trackUserInvalid, adds no second timing source)

the field observes the control via the seam's REACTIVE closures (userInvalid()/validity() — the ADR-0050 §4 surface; coordinator re-ruled after the s11 UA-checkpoint finding) — no polling, no reach into control parts, no event-ordering dependence
field anatomy: label / control slot / description / error as control-owned parts in column flow; STRUCTURE geometry class (no Control-band height); label rides the type ramp
single field.css; :where declares --ui-field-* from roles (danger error ink, muted description); @scope consumes only --ui-field-*; forced-colors + motion discipline hold
ui-field's public surface is recorded in field.md frontmatter and drift-gated (attributes[] ≡ finalize)
field behaviour is probe-enforced (jsdom) with negative controls (pre-interaction suppression; drifted-attribute trip-wire)
field cross-engine smoke — real AX name read-back, error forced-colors survival, the whole-shape bounding box
the provider discovers member controls (present + late-added) and scopes to the NEAREST provider under nesting
the provider exposes a REACTIVE aggregate (values by name · invalid set · overall validity) built as computeds over member signals — fine-grained, zero polling
submit aggregates the registry and emits (house event vocab); composes with an optional native <form>, never creates a native form widget
reset restores members (native form.reset() delegation when wrapped, else formReset) and re-suppresses user-invalid — no post-reset danger flash
ui-form-provider's public surface + the registration protocol are recorded in form-provider.md and drift-gated
provider behaviour is probe-enforced (jsdom) with negative controls (non-form child never registers; nearest-provider nesting)
provider cross-engine smoke — the aggregate tracks a real typed input in both engines

both new controls are documented on the /site — {doc, demo} pages DERIVED from the descriptors, both TOCs (NAV + CARD_GROUPS) enumerate them; the site drift gates (site-coverage · site-toc) and check:site stay green (KNOWN_UNDOCUMENTED stays empty — shipped-but-undocumented is not a legal state)
both controls + the registry trait are consumable via the barrels; marginals within the 22 KB budget; tree-shake clean (field does not drag provider, nor vice versa)
the G7 DoD closes: a provider-coordinated, KEYBOARD-ONLY end-to-end form (button/text-field/checkbox/switch/select in fields under a provider) round-trips in Chromium AND WebKit
the component-reviewer scores BOTH controls ≥4 both axes BEFORE the wave-commit; goals §G7 flipped DONE in the same change
fork 1 (the context/provider mechanism) is recorded as ADR-0050 (proposed → Kim ratifies) and fork 2 (the labelling seam) is decided + recorded before the build proceeds
